steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Boundaries question. The blank ends the embedded quotation and must be followed by the supplementary noun phrase “an apt assessment given that…” The correct punctuation must mark the boundary between the main clause (everything through the closing quotation mark) and that noun phrase without creating an illogical or ungrammatical construction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — materialism"; and: A semicolon joins two independent clauses; “an apt assessment” is a noun phrase, not an independent clause. The conjunction “and” then makes the sentence read as if the novel depicts the upper classes as “an apt assessment,” which is nonsensical. ✗
  • Choice B — materialism" and: The coordinating conjunction “and” without a comma links the main clause to “an apt assessment,” illogically suggesting the novel depicts the upper classes as “an apt assessment.” ✗
  • Choice C — materialism,": A comma after the closing quotation mark correctly separates the main clause from the supplementary noun phrase “an apt assessment,” which then elaborates on the observation without distorting the meaning. ✓
  • Choice D — materialism": No punctuation at all fails to mark the boundary between the main clause and the supplementary noun phrase, creating a run-on. ✗
C is correct.
